---
title: 'π0: A Vision-Language-Action Flow Model for General Robot Control'
description: A VLM expert + a flow-matching action expert in one transformer; action
  chunks are generated by integrating a learned vector field.
categories:
- Robot Learning (VLA)
---

Paper: [arXiv:2410.24164](https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.24164)

## The π0 model

![π0: VLM expert + 300M action expert in one transformer, flow-matching action generation](/images/notes/pi0-1.png)

```text
Observation o_t  →  [ images | language | state ]
        image encoder ↓        ↓ tokenizer
Tokens: [ I₁…Iₙ , ℓ₁…ℓ_m , q_t , a_τ₁…a_τ_H ]
        →  Single Transformer with Dual Experts
             ├─ VLM Expert (pretrained)  → {Image, Language} tokens
             └─ Action Expert (300M)     → {State, Action} tokens
        →  Shared self-attention (Q,K,V mixed; each expert has its own W_QKV)
        →  MLP/LayerNorm per expert  →  action vector field v_θ(A_τ, o_t)
        →  Flow-matching integration (Euler steps)  →  action chunk A_t
```

The observation is $\mathbf{o}_{t} = [\mathbf{I}_{t}^{1}, \dots, \mathbf{I}_{t}^{n}, l_{t}, \mathbf{q}_{t}]$ (images, a language command, joint angles), and the action chunk of $H$ future actions is $\mathbf{A}_{t} = [a_{t}, \dots, a_{t+H-1}]$. The goal is to model $p(\mathbf{A}_{t} \mid \mathbf{o}_{t})$: the **VLM expert** handles image and text, the **action expert** handles proprioception and actions via conditional flow matching:

$$
\mathcal{L}^{\tau}(\theta) = \mathbb{E}\,\big\| \mathbf{v}_{\theta}(\mathbf{A}_{t}^{\tau}, \mathbf{o}_{t}) - \mathbf{u}(\mathbf{A}_{t}^{\tau}, \mathbf{A}_{t}) \big\|^{2}, \quad \tau \in [0, 1]
$$

Flow matching trains a neural ODE $\frac{d\mathbf{A}_t}{d\tau} = \mathbf{v}_\theta(\mathbf{A}_t^\tau, \mathbf{o}_t)$, so integrating from $\tau = 0 \to 1$ turns noise into actions. With the linear interpolation $\mathbf{A}_{t}^{\tau} = \tau\mathbf{A}_{t} + (1-\tau)\epsilon$ (noise $\epsilon \sim \mathcal{N}(0, I)$), the target velocity is simply $\mathbf{u}(\mathbf{A}_{t}^{\tau}, \mathbf{A}_{t}) = \epsilon - \mathbf{A}_{t}$.

**Training step:** sample $(\mathbf{A}_t, \mathbf{o}_t)$, a flow step $\tau \sim \text{Beta}(\alpha, \beta)$, and noise $\epsilon$; build $\mathbf{A}_t^\tau$; and minimise $\|\mathbf{v}_\theta - (\epsilon - \mathbf{A}_t)\|^2$.

**Inference:** start from $\mathbf{A}^{0} \sim \mathcal{N}(0, I)$ and forward-Euler integrate $\mathbf{A}_{t}^{\tau+\delta} = \mathbf{A}_{t}^{\tau} + \delta\,\mathbf{v}_{\theta}(\mathbf{A}_{t}^{\tau}, \mathbf{o}_{t})$ from $\tau = 0 \to 1$.

π0 uses **block-wise causal attention** with three blocks:

- **Block 1 (vision + language)** — tokens $[\mathbf{I}_{t}^{1}, \dots, \mathbf{I}_{t}^{n}, l_{t}]$; cannot see blocks 2–3.
- **Block 2 (proprioceptive state)** — token $[\mathbf{q}_{t}]$; attends to block 1, not block 3; KV-cached.
- **Block 3 (action tokens)** — $[a_{t}, \dots, a_{t+H-1}]$; attends to blocks 1 and 2.
